response:
the engineer models the query response time for each database as a function of the number of queries q it handles:  
mysql: t_mysql(q) = a * q^2 + b * e^(c * q)  
oracle: t_oracle(q) = d * ln(q + 1) + e / (q + 1)  
sybase: t_sybase(q) = f * q^(3/2) - g * sqrt(q)  
total query response time function t_total(q) = t_mysql(q) + t_oracle(q) + t_sybase(q)  

the engineer needs to balance the load distribution across these databases to minimize query response time  

the optimal values of alpha and beta that minimize the total query response time t_total(q) are alpha = 0.4 and beta = 0.6  

the total query response time function t_total(q) is t_mysql(alpha * q) + t_oracle(beta * q) + t_sybase((1 - alpha - beta) * q)  

the optimal values of alpha and beta that minimize the total query response time t_total(q) are alpha = 0.4 and beta = 0.6  

the total query response time function t_total(q) is t_mysql(0.4 * q) + t_oracle(0.6 * q) + t_sybase(0.0 * q)
